annotations.label: Equivalence relation

anchor.snippet: an equivalence relation is a binary relation that is reflexive , symmetric , and transitive

annotations.note: Lean core's `Setoid` bundles a relation with `Equivalence` (reflexive, symmetric, transitive); Mathlib builds extensively on this in `Mathlib/Data/Setoid/Basic.lean`.

annotations.label: Numerical equality as equivalence relation

anchor.snippet: A simpler example is numerical equality

annotations.note: Equality is the canonical equivalence relation; in Mathlib it appears as `Setoid.bot_def` showing the bottom Setoid has relation `(· = ·)`.

annotations.label: Equivalence relation induces partition into equivalence classes

anchor.snippet: Each equivalence relation provides a partition of the underlying set into disjoint equivalence classes

annotations.note: `Setoid.isPartition_classes` states that `r.classes` forms a partition (`IsPartition`).

annotations.label: Equivalence relation (formal)

anchor.snippet: is said to be an equivalence relation if it is reflexive, symmetric and transitive

annotations.note: Same as `Setoid` from Lean core, with `iseqv : Equivalence` packaging reflexivity, symmetry, and transitivity.

annotations.label: Setoid and equivalence class

anchor.snippet: together with the relation

annotations.note: `Setoid.classes` (Mathlib/Data/Setoid/Partition.lean) defines the set of equivalence classes of a `Setoid`.

annotations.label: Composite relation in relational algebra

anchor.snippet: the composite relation

annotations.note: `Relation.Comp` (notation `∘r`) defines composition of binary relations.

annotations.label: Equivalence relation via relational algebra

anchor.snippet: The defining properties of an equivalence relation

annotations.note: Building blocks (`Relation.Comp`, inverse) exist, but the characterization of equivalence via `r⁻¹ = r` and `r ∘r r = r` is not stated as a single lemma.

annotations.label: Simple equivalence example on a finite set

anchor.snippet: is an equivalence relation. The following sets are equivalence classes

annotations.note: This worked example of a concrete equivalence on a small set is not present in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: is an equivalence relation. The following sets are equivalence classes

annotations.label: Quotient set

anchor.snippet: It is also called the quotient set

annotations.note: `Quotient r` (Lean core) is the quotient set of a type by a `Setoid`.

annotations.label: Equality is an equivalence relation

anchor.snippet: "Is equal to" on the set of numbers

annotations.note: `eq_equivalence` proves that equality is an `Equivalence`; instantiated as `⊥ : Setoid α` in `Setoid.bot_def`.

annotations.label: Similarity of triangles

anchor.snippet: "Is similar to" on the set of all triangles

annotations.note: `Similar` defines similarity of indexed point families; `EuclideanGeometry` proves similarity criteria for triangles in `Mathlib/Geometry/Euclidean/Similarity.lean`.

annotations.label: Congruence of triangles

anchor.snippet: "Is congruent to" on the set of all triangles

annotations.note: `Congruent v₁ v₂` (notation `v₁ ≅ v₂`) defines congruence of indexed point families; `Mathlib/Geometry/Euclidean/Congruence.lean` provides triangle SSS, etc.

annotations.label: Same image under a function

anchor.snippet: "has the same image under

annotations.note: `Setoid.ker f` is exactly the equivalence relation `f x = f y`.

annotations.label: Same absolute value

anchor.snippet: "Has the same absolute value as" on the set of real numbers

annotations.note: This specific equivalence is not declared, but `Setoid.ker abs` realizes it via the kernel construction.

annotations.label: Same cosine

anchor.snippet: "Has the same cosine as" on the set of all angles

annotations.note: Not declared explicitly, but expressible as `Setoid.ker Real.cos`.

annotations.label: Congruence modulo n

anchor.snippet: "is congruent to, modulo

annotations.note: `Int.ModEq` and `Nat.ModEq` formalize congruence modulo n with reflexivity/symmetry/transitivity instances.

annotations.label: Equipollence of directed line segments

anchor.snippet: "Have the same length and direction"

annotations.note: The equipollence equivalence relation on directed segments is not declared in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Same birthday

anchor.snippet: "Has the same birthday as" on the set of all people

annotations.note: Informal example; not formalized.

annotations.label: ≥ on reals is not symmetric

anchor.snippet: The relation "≥" between real numbers is reflexive and transitive, but not symmetric

annotations.note: Stated as commentary about non-symmetry, not as a Mathlib lemma.

annotations.label: Common factor relation is not transitive

anchor.snippet: The relation "has a common factor greater than 1 with"

annotations.note: Counter-example commentary not formalized.

annotations.label: Empty relation is not reflexive

anchor.snippet: The empty relation R (defined so that aRb is never true) on a set X is vacuously symmetric and transitive

annotations.note: Counter-example commentary not formalized.

annotations.label: Approximately equal is not transitive

anchor.snippet: The relation "is approximately equal to" between real numbers

annotations.note: Informal counter-example, not formalized.

annotations.label: Partial order

anchor.snippet: A partial order is a relation that is reflexive, antisymmetric , and transitive

annotations.note: `class PartialOrder` extends `Preorder` with `le_antisymm`.

annotations.label: Equality as equivalence and partial order

anchor.snippet: Equality is both an equivalence relation and a partial order

annotations.note: `eq_equivalence` confirms equality is an equivalence; that `(· = ·)` is a partial order is implicit but not packaged as a named lemma.

annotations.label: Strict partial order

anchor.snippet: A strict partial order is irreflexive, transitive, and asymmetric

annotations.note: `IsStrictOrder` bundles `IsIrrefl` and `IsTrans` (asymmetry follows); also represented unbundled by `StrictMono`/`<`.

annotations.label: Partial equivalence relation

anchor.snippet: A partial equivalence relation is transitive and symmetric

annotations.note: Mathlib has `IsSymm` and `IsTrans` separately but no bundled `PartialEquivalence` structure.

annotations.label: Ternary equivalence relation

anchor.snippet: A ternary equivalence relation is a ternary analogue

annotations.note: No ternary equivalence relation is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Dependency / tolerance relation

anchor.snippet: A reflexive and symmetric relation is a dependency relation

annotations.note: No dedicated `ToleranceRelation`/`DependencyRelation` definition exists; only `IsRefl` and `IsSymm` separately.

annotations.label: Preorder

anchor.snippet: A preorder is reflexive and transitive

annotations.note: `class Preorder` extends `LE`, `LT` with `le_refl` and `le_trans`.

annotations.label: Congruence relation

anchor.snippet: A congruence relation is an equivalence relation whose domain

annotations.note: `Con M` is an equivalence relation compatible with multiplication; analogous `AddCon` exists for additive structures.

annotations.label: Equivalence relation as negation of apartness

anchor.snippet: Any equivalence relation is the negation of an apartness relation

annotations.note: Apartness relations are not present in Mathlib, so the negation correspondence is not formalized.

annotations.label: Reflexive Euclidean implies equivalence

anchor.snippet: Each relation that is both reflexive and left (or right) Euclidean is also an equivalence relation

annotations.note: Euclidean relations are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Well-defined / class invariant property

anchor.snippet: the property

annotations.note: Class-invariance is the hypothesis used by `Quotient.lift` (and `Setoid.liftEquiv`), but no separate `ClassInvariant`/`WellDefined` predicate is declared.

annotations.label: Morphism / invariant function

anchor.snippet: is said to be a morphism for

annotations.note: `Setoid.liftEquiv r` identifies `{f // r ≤ Setoid.ker f}` with `Quotient r → β`; the side condition expresses being a morphism for `r`.

annotations.label: Morphism between equivalence relations

anchor.snippet: Such a function is known as a morphism from

annotations.note: `Quotient.map` lifts a function respecting `r → s` to quotients; no separately named `Setoid.Hom` definition exists.

annotations.label: Equivalence class

anchor.snippet: is called an equivalence class of

annotations.note: An element of `Setoid.classes r` is an equivalence class; `Setoid.mem_classes` builds `{x | r x y}` for each `y`.

annotations.label: Quotient set

anchor.snippet: is the quotient set of

annotations.note: `Quotient s` is the quotient set; `Setoid.quotientEquivClasses` exhibits it as bijective with `Setoid.classes`.

annotations.label: Projection onto quotient

anchor.snippet: The projection of

annotations.note: `Quotient.mk r` is the canonical projection onto the quotient.

annotations.label: Theorem on projections

anchor.snippet: Theorem on projections

annotations.note: The universal-property part is `Quotient.lift`/`Setoid.liftEquiv`; the umbrella `Theorem on projections` from the article is not packaged as a single Mathlib result.

annotations.label: Equivalence kernel of a function

anchor.snippet: The equivalence kernel of a function

annotations.note: `Setoid.ker f` is the equivalence relation `f x = f y`.

annotations.label: Equivalence kernel of injection is identity

anchor.snippet: The equivalence kernel of an injection is the identity relation

annotations.note: `Setoid.injective_iff_ker_bot` proves `Injective f ↔ Setoid.ker f = ⊥`, where `⊥` is the equality Setoid.

annotations.label: Partition of a set

anchor.snippet: A partition of X is a set P of nonempty subsets of X

annotations.note: `Setoid.IsPartition c` says `∅ ∉ c` and every element of `α` lies in a unique block.

annotations.label: Number of equivalence relations equals Bell number

anchor.snippet: the number of equivalence relations on X equals the number of distinct partitions of X

annotations.note: `Nat.bell` is defined recursively but `Bell.lean` notes in TODO that it is not yet proved equal to the number of partitions/equivalence relations.

anchors.snippet: the number of equivalence relations on X equals the number of distinct partitions of X

annotations.label: Fundamental theorem of equivalence relations

anchor.snippet: A key result links equivalence relations and partitions

annotations.note: `Setoid.Partition.orderIso` is the order-isomorphism `Setoid α ≃o Partitions α`, the partition–equivalence correspondence.

annotations.label: Coarser and finer equivalence relations

anchor.snippet: is said to be a coarser relation than

annotations.note: `Setoid.le_def`: `r ≤ s ↔ ∀ x y, r x y → s x y` is the finer/coarser order on `Setoid α`.

annotations.label: Finer iff classes are subsets

anchor.snippet: is finer than

annotations.note: `Setoid.Partition.orderIso` is order-preserving, encoding that `r ≤ s` matches refinement of classes; no separate `finer_iff_classes_subset` lemma.

annotations.label: Finest and coarsest equivalence relations

anchor.snippet: The equality equivalence relation is the finest equivalence relation on any set

annotations.note: In `Setoid.completeLattice`, `⊥` is equality (`Setoid.bot_def`) — the finest — and `⊤` is the total relation (`Setoid.top_def`) — the coarsest.

annotations.label: Lattice of equivalence relations is geometric

anchor.snippet: on the collection of all equivalence relations on a fixed set is itself a partial order relation

annotations.note: `Setoid.completeLattice` gives the complete lattice structure on `Setoid α`, but the geometric-lattice / Steinitz exchange property is not formalized.

annotations.label: Equivalence on functions by fixed-point cardinality

anchor.snippet: Two functions are deemed equivalent when their respective sets of fixed points have the same cardinality

annotations.note: This specific equivalence is not declared in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Equivalence relations correspond to surjections

anchor.snippet: is the equivalence kernel of its surjective projection

annotations.note: `Setoid.ker_mk_eq r : Setoid.ker (Quotient.mk'' : α → Quotient r) = r` shows every equivalence relation is the kernel of its canonical surjective projection.

annotations.label: Intersection of equivalence relations

anchor.snippet: The intersection of any collection of equivalence relations over X

annotations.note: The `InfSet (Setoid α)` instance and `Setoid.sInf_def` show that the infimum of a set of equivalence relations is again an equivalence relation.

annotations.label: Equivalence relation generated by a relation

anchor.snippet: the equivalence relation generated by R is the intersection of all equivalence relations containing R

annotations.note: `Setoid.eqvGen_eq` shows `EqvGen.setoid r = sInf {s | r ≤ s}`, i.e. the generated equivalence relation is the intersection of equivalences containing `r`.

annotations.label: Equivalence generated by total order is trivial

anchor.snippet: the equivalence relation generated by any total order on X has exactly one equivalence class

annotations.note: Not formalized as a Mathlib lemma.

annotations.label: Torus as quotient of unit square

anchor.snippet: can be naturally identified

annotations.note: The torus-as-quotient-of-the-square construction is not formalized in this form.

annotations.label: Three connected theorems on equivalences and transformation groups

anchor.snippet: Then the following three connected theorems hold

annotations.note: These bundled theorems linking equivalence relations and transformation groups are not packaged as such in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Cosets as equivalence classes of subgroup action

anchor.snippet: The equivalence classes of ~—also called the orbits of the action of H on G —are the right cosets of H in G

annotations.note: `QuotientGroup` realizes the quotient of a group by a (normal) subgroup as cosets via a Setoid; for the unnormalized version `QuotientGroup.leftRel` gives the coset equivalence.

annotations.label: Groupoid representing an equivalence relation

anchor.snippet: Then we can form a groupoid representing this equivalence relation

annotations.note: Mathlib has `CategoryTheory.Groupoid` but no dedicated construction turning a `Setoid` into the corresponding equivalence groupoid.

annotations.label: Lattice of equivalence relations Con X

anchor.snippet: The equivalence relations on any set X , when ordered by set inclusion , form a complete lattice

annotations.note: `Setoid.completeLattice` instance gives `CompleteLattice (Setoid α)`.

annotations.label: ω-categorical equivalence relation example

anchor.snippet: an equivalence relation with exactly two infinite equivalence classes is an easy example of a theory which is ω- categorical

annotations.note: Mathlib's `ModelTheory` library defines categoricity but does not formalize this specific example.

annotations.label: Independence of equivalence relation axioms

anchor.snippet: the three defining properties of equivalence relations can be proved mutually independent

annotations.note: The independence of reflexivity, symmetry, transitivity is not formalized in Mathlib.
